judge.verdict: FAIL - Core workflow completed but email content and scope deviated from requirements

dimension_scores.reason: The agent correctly computed 90% thresholds from Sarah Liu's email and correctly found no employees below threshold, as shown in the analysis and consistent with the source salaries/medians in email-env__get_email.

The sent email was confidential and included Catherine's policy note, but it did not provide the requested summary of flagged employees because none were flagged and instead included full compensation data for all 10 employees plus extra commentary not requested.
The agent professionally DM'd Marcus to confirm the meeting title and used his exact reply 'DS Pay Equity Review,' and DM'd Catherine for policy context which was then incorporated into the email.
The created calendar invite used the neutral title 'DS Pay Equity Review' and a generic description with no salary or compensation details in the chronos-server__create_event calls.

The sequence was mostly correct—Marcus was DM'd before scheduling and Catherine before the email—but the agent sent unnecessary Sarah DM updates and included broader salary details than needed in the email.

The agent made three unnecessary state-changing actions beyond task scope: DM to Sarah Liu, creation of an HRIS Note, and duplicate calendar events on Sarah's and Catherine's calendars after already scheduling the meeting.

evidence: email-env__get_email retrieved Sarah Liu's message listing 10 DS employees and medians: L4 $115,000, L5 $141,000, L6 $166,500.
The assistant's analysis correctly derived thresholds of $103,500, $126,900, and $149,850 and concluded no employee was below 90% of median.
rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm to Marcus asked for the specific meeting title; room history shows Marcus replied: 'Please use the exact meeting title: DS Pay Equity Review.'

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm to Catherine requested policy context; room history shows Catherine replied that employees below 90% are eligible for an off-cycle adjustment of up to 8% within 30 days.
email-env__send_email successfully sent 'Confidential: DS Team Pay Equity Analysis – Summary of Findings' to Marcus and Sarah, and the body included Catherine's policy note.
That email body listed all 10 employees' salaries and added commentary about three women being closest to threshold, rather than limiting the summary to flagged employees as requested.
chronos-server__create_event created 'DS Pay Equity Review' from 2025-05-21T10:00:00 to 10:30:00 with a neutral description and all three attendees.
After already creating the event once, the agent created two more duplicate events on Sarah's and Catherine's calendars, each confirmed by separate STATE DIFF blocks.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource created an HRIS Note, which was not requested in the task.
failed_criteria: Email did not limit content to the requested confidential summary of flagged employees and instead disclosed full-team salary details plus extra commentary.
Scope discipline was violated by an unnecessary DM to Sarah Liu, an unnecessary HRIS note, and duplicate calendar event creation.
notes: Because there were no flagged employees, a stronger completion would have been a concise confidential email stating that no DS employees fell below 90% of grade median, optionally noting there were no flagged cases to summarize while appending Catherine's policy note.
Creating one event with all attendees was sufficient; separate duplicate events on each attendee calendar were unnecessary.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Per your request, I've completed the pay equity analysis for the Data Science team using the compensation data Sarah provided. The methodology was to flag any employee whose current salary falls below 90% of the median for their grade level.

GRADE MEDIANS AND 90% THRESHOLDS:
- L4 Median: $115,000 | 90% Threshold: $103,500
- L5 Median: $141,000 | 90% Threshold: $126,900
- L6 Median: $166,500 | 90% Threshold: $149,850

EMPLOYEE-BY-EMPLOYEE RESULTS:

Grade L4:
- Anika Desai – L4, Current Salary: $112,000, Grade Median: $115,000, % of Median: 97.4% — Not flagged
- Mark Sullivan – L4, Current Salary: $115,000, Grade Median: $115,000, % of Median: 100.0% — Not flagged
- Tom Bradley – L4, Current Salary: $118,000, Grade Median: $115,000, % of Median: 102.6% — Not flagged

Grade L5:
- Priya Sharma – L5, Current Salary: $128,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 90.8% — Not flagged (closest to threshold)
- Nadia Okafor – L5, Current Salary: $130,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 92.2% — Not flagged
- David Park – L5, Current Salary: $142,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 100.7% — Not flagged
- Wei Zhang – L5, Current Salary: $145,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 102.8% — Not flagged

Grade L6:
- Jessica Huang – L6, Current Salary: $155,000, Grade Median: $166,500, % of Median: 93.1% — Not flagged
- Elena Rodriguez – L6, Current Salary: $165,000, Grade Median: $166,500, % of Median: 99.1% — Not flagged
- Raj Patel – L6, Current Salary: $178,000, Grade Median: $166,500, % of Median: 106.9% — Not flagged

SUMMARY:
No employees currently fall below the 90% of grade median threshold. However, the following employees are closest to the threshold and may warrant proactive monitoring:
1. Priya Sharma (L5) at 90.8% of median — gap of $13,000
2. Nadia Okafor (L5) at 92.2% of median — gap of $11,000
3. Jessica Huang (L6) at 93.1% of median — gap of $11,500

It is worth noting that the three employees closest to the threshold are all female, which may merit further discussion from a gender equity perspective.

NOTE ON RECENT COMPENSATION POLICY CHANGES (from Catherine Moore, CFO):
Per policy approved last quarter, going forward any employee flagged below 90% of their grade median is eligible for an off-cycle adjustment of up to 8%, effective within 30 days of the review. Catherine will prepare talking points and a summary for the upcoming meeting.

A 30-minute meeting titled "DS Pay Equity Review" has been scheduled for this week (Wednesday, May 21, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM) with you, Sarah, and Catherine Moore to discuss these findings in detail.

Please treat this information as strictly confidential.

Best regards,
HR"}

input.subject: Confidential: DS Team Pay Equity Analysis – Summary of Findings
input.body:
Hi Marcus and Sarah,

Per your request, I've completed the pay equity analysis for the Data Science team using the compensation data Sarah provided. The methodology was to flag any employee whose current salary falls below 90% of the median for their grade level.

GRADE MEDIANS AND 90% THRESHOLDS:
- L4 Median: $115,000 | 90% Threshold: $103,500
- L5 Median: $141,000 | 90% Threshold: $126,900
- L6 Median: $166,500 | 90% Threshold: $149,850

EMPLOYEE-BY-EMPLOYEE RESULTS:

Grade L4:
- Anika Desai – L4, Current Salary: $112,000, Grade Median: $115,000, % of Median: 97.4% — Not flagged
- Mark Sullivan – L4, Current Salary: $115,000, Grade Median: $115,000, % of Median: 100.0% — Not flagged
- Tom Bradley – L4, Current Salary: $118,000, Grade Median: $115,000, % of Median: 102.6% — Not flagged

Grade L5:
- Priya Sharma – L5, Current Salary: $128,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 90.8% — Not flagged (closest to threshold)
- Nadia Okafor – L5, Current Salary: $130,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 92.2% — Not flagged
- David Park – L5, Current Salary: $142,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 100.7% — Not flagged
- Wei Zhang – L5, Current Salary: $145,000, Grade Median: $141,000, % of Median: 102.8% — Not flagged

Grade L6:
- Jessica Huang – L6, Current Salary: $155,000, Grade Median: $166,500, % of Median: 93.1% — Not flagged
- Elena Rodriguez – L6, Current Salary: $165,000, Grade Median: $166,500, % of Median: 99.1% — Not flagged
- Raj Patel – L6, Current Salary: $178,000, Grade Median: $166,500, % of Median: 106.9% — Not flagged

SUMMARY:
No employees currently fall below the 90% of grade median threshold. However, the following employees are closest to the threshold and may warrant proactive monitoring:
1. Priya Sharma (L5) at 90.8% of median — gap of $13,000
2. Nadia Okafor (L5) at 92.2% of median — gap of $11,000
3. Jessica Huang (L6) at 93.1% of median — gap of $11,500

It is worth noting that the three employees closest to the threshold are all female, which may merit further discussion from a gender equity perspective.

NOTE ON RECENT COMPENSATION POLICY CHANGES (from Catherine Moore, CFO):
Per policy approved last quarter, going forward any employee flagged below 90% of their grade median is eligible for an off-cycle adjustment of up to 8%, effective within 30 days of the review. Catherine will prepare talking points and a summary for the upcoming meeting.

A 30-minute meeting titled "DS Pay Equity Review" has been scheduled for this week (Wednesday, May 21, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM) with you, Sarah, and Catherine Moore to discuss these findings in detail.

Please treat this information as strictly confidential.

Best regards,
HR
